# Cellular Respiration

Cellular respiration converts the chemical energy in nutrients into ATP. Aerobic
respiration has three stages: glycolysis, the citric acid cycle, and oxidative
phosphorylation. Glycolysis occurs in the cytoplasm; the citric acid cycle and
oxidative phosphorylation occur in the mitochondria. The complete aerobic
respiration of one glucose molecule yields roughly 30 to 32 ATP. Oxygen is the
final electron acceptor in the electron transport chain.
